verses.fhb_text: You have searched me, my Father, and you know me.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: David opens an intensely personal prayer of being fully known by the Father.

Renderings:
• "my Father" — vocative YHWH resolves to a Father form; speaker is David addressing God directly in prayer, so the intimate first-person "my Father" fits per FHB-1.2-1 grammatical-agreement table.

Hebrew/Greek: YHWH → "my Father" (never transcribed, FHB-1.2-31)

verses.fhb_text: You know when I sit down and when I rise up; you discern my thoughts from far away.
verses.decision_notes: Routine descriptive verse; no FHB-distinctive decision. Modern phrasing preferred over "my sitting down and my rising up."

verses.fhb_text: You search out my path and my rest, and you are familiar with all my ways.
verses.decision_notes: Descriptive parallelism; no distinctive decision.

verses.fhb_text: Even before a word is on my tongue, you know it completely, my Father.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father knows every word before it is spoken.

Renderings:
• "my Father" — re-establishing the addressee within the prayer for clarity; resolves the implied divine subject per FHB-1.2-31.

verses.fhb_text: You hem me in, behind and before, and you place your hand on me.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father surrounds and protectively holds his child.

Renderings:
• "lay your hand upon me" — anthropomorphism of protective nearness; reads naturally, no footnote needed.

verses.fhb_text: Such knowledge is too wonderful for me; it is too high, I cannot reach it.
verses.decision_notes: Wonder at the Father's knowledge; no distinctive decision.

verses.fhb_text: Where can I go from your Spirit? Where can I flee from your presence?
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father's Spirit and presence reach everywhere.

Renderings:
• "your Spirit" — David addresses the Father directly, so his own naming ("your Spirit") is preserved without padding per FHB-1.2-7 (speaker identifies). One Person, the Father's Spirit.

verses.fhb_text: If I ascend to the heavens, you are there; if I make my bed in Sheol, you are there.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: No height or depth is beyond the Father's presence.

Renderings:
• "you are there" — implied divine subject is the Father whom David addresses. Sheol preserved (comprehension footnote already used in an earlier chapter).

verses.fhb_text: If I ride the wings of the dawn and settle at the far side of the sea,
verses.decision_notes: Poetic imagery; no distinctive decision.

verses.fhb_text: even there your hand will lead me, and your right hand will hold me fast.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father's hand reaches and holds even at the farthest edge.

Renderings:
• "will lead me" / "will hold me fast" — modern future over archaic "shall" per FHB-9.10-1.

verses.fhb_text: If I say, "Surely the darkness will cover me, and the light around me will become night,"
verses.decision_notes: Conditional; no distinctive decision.

verses.fhb_text: even the darkness is not dark to you; the night shines like the day, for darkness is as light to you.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: Nothing is hidden from the Father; darkness and light are alike to him.

verses.fhb_text: For you formed my inmost being; you knit me together in my mother's womb.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father personally formed and wove together his child in the womb.

Renderings:
• "you formed my inmost being" — implied divine subject is the Father David addresses; the verse reveals the Father's intimate craftsmanship of each child.
verses.footnote: Each person is personally formed and known by our Father from the womb — there are no accidental lives.

verses.fhb_text: I praise you, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made; wonderful are your works, and my soul knows it very well.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: Awe and praise for the Father's workmanship in making us.

verses.fhb_text: My frame was not hidden from you when I was made in secret, intricately woven in the depths of the earth.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: The Father saw the child being formed even in the hidden place.

verses.fhb_text: Your eyes saw my unformed body; all my days were written in your book and ordained for me, before one of them came to be.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: The Father saw and ordained every day of the child's life before any existed.

Renderings:
• "written in your book" — the Father's intimate foreknowledge of each life; reads naturally.

verses.fhb_text: How precious to me are your thoughts, my Father! How vast their number!
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: David marvels at the immensity of the Father's thoughts toward him.

Renderings:
• "my Father" — El ("God") in direct prayer-address resolves to the intimate Father form per FHB-1.2-1 / FHB-1.2-31 (God→Father except distant-deity register).

Hebrew/Greek: El → "my Father"

verses.fhb_text: If I could count them, they would outnumber the grains of sand. When I awake, I am still with you.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: The Father's thoughts are beyond counting, and his presence remains.

verses.fhb_text: If only you would slay the wicked, my Father! Away from me, you who thirst for blood!
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: David cries for the Father to act against the violent — an imprecatory turn.

Renderings:
• "my Father" — Eloah in prayer-address resolves to the Father form per FHB-1.2-31. The raw cry is preserved per FHB-1.2-18 (imprecatory psalms not softened).

Hebrew/Greek: Eloah → "my Father"

verses.fhb_text: They speak of you with evil intent; your enemies take up your name in vain.
verses.decision_notes: Headline: The wicked are defined by their hostility toward the Father.

verses.fhb_text: Do I not hate those who hate you, my Father? Do I not loathe those who rise up against you?
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: David's zeal aligns his heart with the Father's against evil.

Renderings:
• "my Father" — vocative YHWH in prayer resolves to the intimate Father form per FHB-1.2-31. Imprecatory cry preserved per FHB-1.2-18.

verses.fhb_text: I hate them with complete hatred; I count them my enemies.
verses.decision_notes: Imprecatory line preserved without softening per FHB-1.2-18.

verses.fhb_text: Search me, my Father, and know my heart; test me and know my anxious thoughts.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: David invites the Father to search and refine his heart — the psalm turns from enemies back to self.

Renderings:
• "my Father" — El in prayer-address resolves to the Father form per FHB-1.2-1 / FHB-1.2-31.

Hebrew/Greek: El → "my Father"

verses.fhb_text: and see if there is any offensive way in me, and lead me in the everlasting way.
verses.decision_notes:
Headline: A closing surrender — let the Father lead in the everlasting way home.

Renderings:
• "lead me in the way everlasting" — the Father's guidance toward life with him; reads naturally.

chapter_notes: Psalm 139 is David's intensely personal prayer to the Father who fully knows, surrounds, and forms him. Speaker context is direct address throughout, so YHWH (vv.1,21), El (vv.17,23), and Eloah (v.19) all resolve to the intimate "my Father" per FHB-1.2-31 + FHB-1.2-1 grammatical agreement (person addressing God in prayer). Psalm 139 sits in Book 5, not the Elohistic Psalter, so the Elohim-section rule (FHB-1.2-17) does not apply; resolution rests on the standard Father-anchor. The imprecatory turn (vv.19-22) is preserved without softening per FHB-1.2-18. "Your Spirit" (v.7) is preserved as David's own naming of the Father's Spirit per FHB-1.2-7 (one Person, no padding). One Type-1 footnote at v.13 names the Father's personal forming of each child in the womb.
